content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of **n** integers **a1**, **a2**, ..., **an** and an integer **d**.

Find the length of the shortest non-empty contiguous subsequence with sum of elements at least **d**. Formally, you should find the smallest positive integer **k** with the following property: there is an integer **s** (1 ≤ **s** ≤ **N-k+1**) such that **as + as+1 + ... + as+k-1** ≥ **d**.
constraints: 1 ≤ sum of n over all test cases ≤ 2 * 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers n and d. The second line contains n space-separated integers a1, a2, ..., an.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the length of the shortest contiguous subsequence with sum of elements ≥ d. If there is no such subsequence, print -1 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the shortest subarray with sum greater than or equal to 5 is [2, 3]. For the second test case, the shortest subarray with sum greater than or equal to 1 is [1].
There is no subarray with sum greater than or equal to 10.
The shortest subarray with sum greater than or equal to 15 is [5, 5, 5].

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use the sliding window technique along with prefix sums. We calculate the prefix sum of the array and then use two pointers to represent the sliding window. We keep expanding the window to the right until the sum of the elements within the window is greater than or equal to d. We then try to minimize the window by moving the left pointer to the right while keeping the sum greater than or equal to d. We keep track of the minimum length of the window that satisfies the condition.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * n) — where T is the number of test cases and n is the maximum length of the array. This is because for each test case, we are potentially scanning the entire array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — for storing the prefix sum array.
